new_classes.label_en: Other Still Wine of Fresh Grapes (no PDO/PGI) in containers > 2 L with actual alcoholic strength > 13% vol and ≤ 15% vol (CN 2204 29 96)
new_classes.label_de: Anderer Stillwein aus frischen Weintrauben (ohne g.U./g.g.A.) in Behältnissen > 2 L mit einem vorhandenen Alkoholgehalt > 13 % vol und ≤ 15 % vol (KN 2204 29 96)
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes without a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO) or Protected Geographical Indication (PGI), presented in containers of more than 2 litres, with an actual alcoholic strength by volume greater than 13% vol and not exceeding 15% vol, as described in CN subheading 2204 29 96. The total dry extract includes non-volatile constituents such as sugars, glycerol, tannins, tartaric acid, colouring matter and salts, as specified in the additional notes 4 and 5 to chapter 22.

coverage_explanation: The primary distinguishing criteria for CN 2204 29 96 are captured: (1) wine of fresh grapes (parent class WineOfFreshGrapes), (2) no PDO/PGI (complement restrictions on PDOOriginQuality and PGIOriginQuality), (3) container volume > 2 L (someValuesFrom LargeContainer), and (4) actual alcoholic strength > 13% vol and ≤ 15% vol (decimal range restrictions on alcoholByVolumePercent). The total dry extract quality is referenced via someValuesFrom TotalDryExtractQuality to acknowledge its definitional relevance per additional notes 4 and 5. Coverage is not 1.0 because: (a) the detailed enumeration of non-volatile constituents of total dry extract (sugars, glycerol, tannins, tartaric acid, colouring matter, salts) from additional note 4(A) cannot be fully expressed in OWL DL without introducing many new part/quality classes; (b) the non-sparkling (still) character (isCarbonated = false) is not captured as a formal restriction since isCarbonated is defined on xsd:boolean and the TBox does not provide a dedicated quality class for stillness at this level; and (c) the cross-reference to additional notes 4 and 5 covers a range of subheadings (22042922–22042998) whose collective scope cannot be uniquely assigned to this one node without risking over-generalisation.
